steps.title: Spot the defining evidence
steps.content: The single decisive clue is that forest plots with limited logging are “more robust than plots that haven't been logged at all.” This finding anchors everything the answer must follow from.

steps.title: Match the implication
steps.content:
If limited logging produces more robust forests, then limited logging must support forest health—which is what A directly states.

B, C, and D contradict or miss this by claiming other strategies work better, introducing doubt the evidence doesn't warrant, or arguing for untouched forests that the study shows underperform.

That is the correct answer.
